patterns.name: Identical ShutDown()/Init() lifecycle methods across KVClient and ObjectClient
patterns.rationale: KVClient::ShutDown and ObjectClient::ShutDown have byte-identical bodies (TraceGuard + impl_ null-check + ShutDown + CompleteHandler), and their Init() bodies are likewise identical (TraceGuard + InitKvMetrics + Init + CompleteHandler). The two client wrappers delegate to the same ObjectClientImpl, so this lifecycle boilerplate is copy-pasted per client. A shared helper or common base would remove the duplication and prevent the two copies from drifting.

patterns.name: Duplicated object_cache::FullParam initialization in KVClient::Create and KVClient::MCreate
patterns.rationale: The six-line FullParam initializer (writeMode/ttlSecond/consistencyType=CAUSAL/cacheType/existence copied from SetParam) is identical in Create and MCreate. Any new SetParam field must be added in both places; a single helper that builds a FullParam from a SetParam would centralize it.

patterns.name: Duplicated RequestParam field mapping for AccessRecorder in KVClient
patterns.rationale: The RequestParam mapping block (objectKey substr + writeMode/ttlSecond/existence/cacheType to_string casts) is repeated verbatim in Create and MCreate (and closely echoed in the Set variants). This access-record population is copy-pasted rather than factored into a helper that fills a RequestParam from a SetParam/FullParam.
